Organizations increasingly experience vulnerabilities outside their sphere of influence in an increasingly interconnected economy. A single cyber attack on a supplier can cause a chain reaction with major consequences. The examples below show how vulnerable our processes are – and why chain safety is crucial. 1.

metadata.source_title: Preventing disastrous halts in the chain

text: A chain is only as strong as its weakest digital link. Organizations increasingly experience vulnerabilities outside their sphere of influence in an increasingly interconnected economy. A single cyber attack on a supplier can cause a chain reaction with major consequences. The examples below show how vulnerable our processes are – and why chain safety is crucial. 1. Food production halted by cyber attack on glass jar supplier: An attack hits a supplier, which stops supplying glass packaging to the food manufacturer.

metadata.source_title: Preventing disastrous halts in the chain

text:
Organizations increasingly experience vulnerabilities outside their sphere of influence in an increasingly interconnected economy. A single cyber attack on a supplier can cause a chain reaction with major consequences. The examples below show how vulnerable our processes are – and why chain safety is crucial. 1. Food production halted by cyber attack on glass jar supplier: An attack hits a supplier, which stops supplying glass packaging to the food manufacturer. The result: empty supermarket shelves and millions in reputational damage.
2.

metadata.source_title: Preventing disastrous halts in the chain

text:
A single cyber attack on a supplier can cause a chain reaction with major consequences. The examples below show how vulnerable our processes are – and why chain safety is crucial. 1. Food production halted by cyber attack on glass jar supplier: An attack hits a supplier, which stops supplying glass packaging to the food manufacturer. The result: empty supermarket shelves and millions in reputational damage.
2. Hospital operations postponed due to hacked laundry: A [ransomware](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/cybersecurity/wat-is-ransomware.html) attack shuts down a hospital laundry.

metadata.source_title: Preventing disastrous halts in the chain

text:
The examples below show how vulnerable our processes are – and why chain safety is crucial. 1. Food production halted by cyber attack on glass jar supplier: An attack hits a supplier, which stops supplying glass packaging to the food manufacturer. The result: empty supermarket shelves and millions in reputational damage.
2. Hospital operations postponed due to hacked laundry: A [ransomware](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/cybersecurity/wat-is-ransomware.html) attack shuts down a hospital laundry. Hospitals no longer receive sterile sheets, which means operations have to be postponed.

metadata.source_title: Preventing disastrous halts in the chain

text:

Hospitals no longer receive sterile sheets, which means operations have to be postponed. The impact on patient safety is immediately noticeable.

Hospitals no longer receive sterile sheets, which means operations have to be postponed. The impact on patient safety is immediately noticeable.

text: The damage to equipment and turnover amounts to millions. All Hazards: think in scenarios – and take action. That is why an [All Hazards approach](https://samendigitaalveilig.nl/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/All-hazards-examples.jpg) is essential: thinking through scenarios, identifying risks, assessing suppliers and taking timely measures.

The role of the NIS2 guideline in chain security.

metadata.source_title: Preventing disastrous halts in the chain

text: Examples show that a cyber attack often affects more than just the affected organization. The impact is chain-wide – and can even lead to social disruption. That is why an [All Hazards approach](https://samendigitaalveilig.nl/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/All-hazards-examples.jpg) is essential: thinking through scenarios, identifying risks, assessing suppliers and taking timely measures. The role of the NIS2 guideline in chain security. Essential and important organizations must examine their entire supply chain due to the European NIS2 directive.

metadata.source_title: Preventing disastrous halts in the chain

text: The impact is chain-wide – and can even lead to social disruption. That is why an [All Hazards approach](https://samendigitaalveilig.nl/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/All-hazards-examples.jpg) is essential: thinking through scenarios, identifying risks, assessing suppliers and taking timely measures. The role of the NIS2 guideline in chain security. Essential and important organizations must examine their entire supply chain due to the European NIS2 directive. According to Article 21.2d of the directive, companies must identify risks in the chain and impose security requirements on suppliers.

metadata.source_title: Preventing disastrous halts in the chain

text: That is why an [All Hazards approach](https://samendigitaalveilig.nl/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/All-hazards-examples.jpg) is essential: thinking through scenarios, identifying risks, assessing suppliers and taking timely measures. The role of the NIS2 guideline in chain security. Essential and important organizations must examine their entire supply chain due to the European NIS2 directive. According to Article 21.2d of the directive, companies must identify risks in the chain and impose security requirements on suppliers. This means a major boost for chain safety in many sectors.
